name: Kip Filet with Sherry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the juniper berries in a bowl. Crush them with the back of a spoon.
2. Stir in the soy sauce, salt, pepper, and sherry.
3. Lay the kip filets in the sauce. Let marinate about 4 hours in the fridge, turning occasionally.
4. Remove the chicken from the marinade (reserving marinade) and dry with kitchen towels.
5. Brush on 1/2 tablespoon oil. Grill over hot coals until done, about 10 minutes.
6. Meanwhile, mix cornstarch with 2 tablespoons cold water to form a smooth mixture.
7. In a saucepan, mix the reserved marinade with 1-1/2 deciliter water. Bring to a boil.
8. Stir in the cornstarch mixture; stir constantly until the sauce thickens.
9. Adjust seasoning as needed.
10. Remove the chicken from the grill and let rest on a cutting board for about 3 minutes.
11. Cut crosswise into pieces about 1/2 cm thick.
12. Arrange the slices on two plates. Pour the sauce over.
13. Serve with rice and lemon carrots.
